title: Implement console streaming via Tactus event bridge
description: Implement DB-backed streaming assistant responses for /lab/console by bridging Tactus agent stream events into ChatMessage upserts and rendering incremental updates in ConversationViewer.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Started implementation. Scope: DB-backed assistant streaming for /lab/console only. Working areas: procedure_executor log bridge, trace sink streaming upsert semantics, ProcedureChatRecorder update_message mutation, ConversationViewer onUpdate incremental state updates.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented DB-backed console streaming bridge and committed as e255cde6. Backend: added _PlexusTraceLogBridge with supports_streaming=true in procedure_executor, ProcedureChatRecorder.update_message mutation path, and PlexusTraceSink streaming semantics (agent_stream_chunk upsert/update, agent_turn completion finalization, placeholder suppression, flush/end_session finalization). Frontend: ConversationViewer now processes ChatMessage.onUpdate and applies in-place message updates by id; polling reduced to fallback cadence. Added UI streaming test: conversation-viewer-streaming-updates.test.tsx. Validation: pytest -q plexus/cli/procedure/test_trace_sink.py plexus/cli/procedure/test_procedure_executor_compat.py (16 passed); npm test -- --runInBand components/ui/__tests__/conversation-viewer-session-routing.test.tsx components/ui/__tests__/conversation-viewer-streaming-updates.test.tsx (2 passed).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Post-merge QA follow-up: user observed slow streaming cadence, unnecessary 'New chat session created' toast, and occasional 'System' badge after assistant output. Reopening for polish/bugfix pass.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Applied QA follow-up patch (not yet re-closed): removed new-session success toast in ConversationViewer, suppressed non-operational role badges so stray 'System' badge no longer renders for plain message rows, and added stream-update coalescing in PlexusTraceSink (min interval/char delta with completion flush) to reduce per-token DB write amplification that was slowing perceived streaming. Also broadened placeholder suppression for 'Assistant turn completed.' regardless of role. Validation: pytest compat suites 16 passed; dashboard conversation viewer tests 2 passed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Additional tuning after user report of word-by-word pauses: adjusted trace streaming coalescing policy to write fewer chunk updates (min chars delta 48, max interval 1.2s, punctuation flush) so AppSync update cadence is less granular and less latency-amplified. This should remove the per-word stagger effect from DB-backed transport. Tests still green: pytest compat suites 16 passed; dashboard conversation tests 2 passed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting dedicated latency instrumentation pass for console streaming UX. Goal: measure (1) submit->first stream chunk latency and (2) inter-chunk cadence, across frontend and backend, with metrics visible/queryable directly by engineering without manual user copy-paste.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented end-to-end latency instrumentation scaffold. Frontend now writes client send/dispatch timing markers into console task metadata (console_chat.instrumentation) at message send + task create + local dispatch request phases. Backend trace sink now writes per-stream timing markers into assistant message metadata (streaming.timings): chunk_count, first/last chunk receive, first persisted chunk, inter-chunk avg/max, backend execute start, runtime execute start, and dispatch queued/client timing passthrough. Added public accessor in ProcedureChatRecorder for latest console task metadata and wired runtime start marker from procedure_executor. Added script scripts/console_stream_latency_report.py to query/report these timings directly from persisted data. Validation: pytest (trace/procedure/chat_recorder suites) 29 passed; dashboard conversation tests 2 passed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation of async GraphQL + SQS + Lambda container path for Console runs. Sequence: add startConsoleRun mutation + resolver, add queue/worker stack, move frontend dispatch to mutation, extend timing diagnostics and tests.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting Amplify-native async migration for Console run dispatch per approved plan. Implementation order: (1) add GraphQL startConsoleRun mutation + resolver that validates and enqueues, (2) add dedicated SQS+Lambda worker to execute Tactus programmatically and persist streaming updates, (3) move frontend dispatch from /api/console/procedure-run to GraphQL mutation, (4) extend latency report script with percentile stage analysis. Baseline latency reference: run f025d8c5-550a-4959-9636-04b7e948eb6d shows dominant dispatch_queued->backend_exec_start (~45s).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented async Console run path wiring:
- Added GraphQL mutation startConsoleRun (Amplify data schema) with resolver dashboard/amplify/data/resolvers/startConsoleRun.ts.
- Resolver validates session/procedure, creates a Task with console_chat metadata + run id, enqueues SQS payload, and returns runId/taskId/accepted/queuedAt.
- Added new Amplify stack ConsoleRunWorkerStack with dedicated SQS queue + DLQ + Lambda container worker (dashboard/amplify/functions/consoleRunWorker/*).
- Worker consumes queue jobs and executes Tactus programmatically via run_experiment_with_task_tracking (no per-send CLI spawn), with task metadata timing markers: queue_enqueued_at, worker_dequeued_at, worker_init_done_at, runtime_init_done_at, completion/failure markers.
- Switched Console frontend dispatch in ConversationViewer from /api/console/procedure-run to startConsoleRun mutation for both prompt sends and HITL resumes.
- Extended trace sink timings to include persisted-update cadence (persisted_update_count, persisted_inter_update_average_ms, persisted_inter_update_max_ms, last_chunk_persisted_at).
- Extended scripts/console_stream_latency_report.py to compute/print percentile summaries and auto-identify dominant latency phase.

Validation:
- Jest: conversation viewer session-routing + streaming-updates + console-dashboard: pass (6 tests).
- Pytest: trace/procedure-executor/chat-recorder suites: pass (29 tests).
- ESLint on changed dashboard files: no errors (2 pre-existing hook-deps warnings in conversation-viewer.tsx).
- python -m py_compile for worker + latency script: pass.

Known follow-up:
- Full dashboard typecheck is noisy/blocking in this workspace due existing project-level TS/CDK dependency issues unrelated to this scope; targeted test/lint coverage above is green.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Staging hotfix implemented in clean worktree from c13e408b. Added missing dashboard modules for console and AI-elements conversation/prompt-input, simplified ai-elements message component to remove undeclared streamdown/ai dependency requirements, aligned task-dispatch/action typing and activity-dashboard embedding props used by Console, removed dashboard ajv override, regenerated lockfile, and updated streaming tests for chunk-coalescing semantics. Validation passed: dashboard typecheck, dashboard ci:unit, dashboard storybook build, and full python pytest.

comments.text: Committed staging hotfix as 98c64774 and pushed to origin/staging. CI run 23696745055 completed SUCCESS. All jobs green (Dashboard Unit Tests, Dashboard Storybook Tests, Python 3.11/3.12) and Trigger AWS CodePipeline job succeeded.

comments.author: ryan

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Root cause confirmed from Amplify job 56 logs: ampx pipeline-deploy fails while building Docker asset ConsoleRunWorkerFunction/AssetImage. Implementing fix to package worker as non-container Lambda asset compatible with Amplify Gen2 backend deploy.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Investigating user-reported Amplify staging deploy failure after CI success. Confirmed failure at ampx pipeline-deploy Docker asset build for ConsoleRunWorkerFunction/AssetImage. Preparing hotfix to bypass containerized worker dependency in backend deploy path so staging can pass while preserving console run dispatch via existing task-dispatch fallback.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Hotfix committed and pushed to staging: 1c123c46 (amplify: unblock staging deploy without console worker image). Change removes ConsoleRunWorkerStack wiring from amplify backend and makes startConsoleRun queue enqueue conditional; when queue URL is absent, resolver now records dispatch_mode=task_dispatcher instead of hard-failing. This is intended to unblock Amplify backend deploy while preserving Console send path via existing task-dispatch fallback.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Follow-up findings and pivot: Amplify staging build image has no docker/dockerd binaries (job 61 logs), so DockerImageFunction assets cannot build in this pipeline. Implemented non-docker console worker bundle path in dashboard/amplify/functions/consoleRunWorker/resource.ts using local Python bundling via requirements file and copied plexus source. Worker implementation in dashboard/amplify/functions/consoleRunWorker/app.py now consumes console SQS jobs, marks task RUNNING/DISPATCHED with instrumentation metadata, and dispatches procedure command through Celery queue. Added worker requirements: boto3, celery, kombu, gql requests transport. Also updated plexus/cli/shared to use lazy imports and moved pandas import inside select_sample_csv to avoid import-time pandas dependency in worker runtime. Validation: py_compile and dashboard typecheck pass; local simulated bundle imports app successfully and bundle size is about 64MB.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Autonomous validation + tuning summary:
- Kept canonical async worker path intact and continued staged latency iteration.
- Commits pushed: 73f0d5ac (0.12/4 -> 0.08/2 experiment), 174f9f8e (same aggressive defaults), then rollback commit 022dffc3 (restore 0.12/4 defaults).
- Deployed and benchmarked on staging repeatedly via scripts/console_auth_smoke.py.

Current validated performance (staging, authenticated smoke):
- Short-turn first assistant delay (12-run final readiness): avg 2.302s, p50 2.342s, p90 2.862s, p95 2.862s, max 3.088s.
- Stability pass (20 runs): avg 2.318s, p95 2.769s, max 3.068s, 0/20 over 6s.
- Long-turn streaming (6 runs, 140-word prompt): first-delay avg 2.271s (p95 2.559s); visible update cadence avg gap 0.593s (p95 0.679s), avg max-gap 0.803s.

Conclusion:
- 0.08/2 increased write pressure and did not improve user-visible cadence consistently.
- 0.12/4 remains the best speed/stability tradeoff and is now restored in code + live env.

Infra deploy note:
- Started infra execution 92855321-6e33-48d3-aae2-0fcfc55be8db for latest staging head; live worker env is already set to 0.12/4 and validated.
